title: Millennium Falsehood

{{Quote|But, since the ''Falcon'' isn't packed with explosives, you'd never send her into a crash dive into the side of a Super Star Destroyer. With this hunk of junk, you wouldn't feel any such compunctions.|[[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]]|<ref name="X-Wing: Solo Command">''[[X-Wing: Solo Command]]''</ref>}}
The '''''Millennium Falsehood''''' was a modified [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300]] [[Light freighter/Legends|light freighter]].

As part of his campaign against [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]], [[General/Legends|General]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] authorized [[Wraith Squadron]] to create a fake [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] to lure the Warlord into a trap, as the genuine ''Falcon'' was stored aboard ''[[Rebel Dream]]'' at the time.

A crew of [[Corellian/Legends|Corellians]] including Wraith pilot [[Myn Donos]], [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] pilot [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]], and Wraith chief mechanic [[Cubber Daine]] along with Rogue Squadron quartermaster [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[M-3PO military protocol droid|M-3PO]] took a [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] to a scrapyard in the [[Corellian system/Legends|Corellian system]] where YT-1300s were cheapest and easiest to find.

They traded a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]-equipped [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptor]] that [[Shalla Nelprin]] acquired from ''[[Razor's Kiss]]'', and returned to ''[[Mon Remonda]]'' with a battered ship that Solo declared was the "ugliest ship I think I've ever seen." [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] worked on the ''Falsehood'' to make it cosmetically similar to the ''Falcon'', by painting over rusted spots and painting false rust on clean hull plates to match the surface patterns. It didn't have the ''Falcon''{{'s}} speed, but it did have similar armament. Also, the ''Falsehood'' was barely spaceworthy when it was acquired. Rumor was that it was acquired from a local junk dealer, which supports the fact that it probably came from a junkyard.

On the ''Falsehood''{{'s}} first mission, Wedge Antilles and Chewbacca landed on [[Kidriff 5]]. Antilles forgot that he didn't speak [[Shyriiwook/Legends|Shyriiwook]], and had to bring Wraith Squadron's droid quartermaster [[Squeaky (droid)|Squeaky]] on the trip to translate. The trip went according to plan, except for [[Gara Petothel]]'s defection to Zsinj, though Rogue [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]] did describe the ''Millennium Falsehood'' as a "flying bathtub."

Though Petothel may have compromised the ''Falsehood'' scheme, Antilles elected to continue the plan. Most of the freighter's missions consisted of stealthy insertions followed by attention-grabbing departures. Zsinj believed the ship really was the ''Falcon'', and it frustrated him.

On an insertion into [[Comkin V]], the ''Falsehood'' was fitted with hull plating to make it appear more like a [[YT-2400 light freighter/Legends|YT-2400]] to sensors. In addition, that plating concealed an [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|A-wing]] and a large explosive device. Both of these hidden surprises were used to great effect during the ship's departure from the planet, as Zsinj deployed the [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|Dreadnaught]] [[Reprisal (Dreadnaught-class)|''Reprisal'']] to intercept the freighter. Gara Petothel was flying with Zsinj's [[Zsinj's impostor 181st Imperial Fighter Group|fake]] [[181st Imperial Fighter Wing/Legends|181st Imperial Fighter Wing]], and trying desperately to avoid harming any [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] forces, so she deliberately collided with the ''Falsehood''.

Neither side took major losses in the Comkin engagement, and ''Millennium Falsehood'' kept flying. Her next appearance was scheduled to be in the [[Vahaba system/Legends|Vahaba system]], but Zsinj brought his fleet into the system before the ''Falsehood'' launched.

The ''Millennium Falsehood'' was not seen after the [[Battle of Vahaba]], but she presumably remained in the possession of the New Republic. Han Solo planned to recommend that the ship be placed in a museum, mainly to stop the requests for him to donate the genuine ''Millennium Falcon''.
